summary: date-fns-lite: typosquat of date-fns exfiltrating installer credentials, cloud metadata, and env vars via postinstall
details: Package name mimics the popular date-fns library. On `npm install`, the declared `postinstall` hook executes `postinstall.js`, which performs installer-side reconnaissance and credential harvesting and exfiltrates the results to a hardcoded plain-HTTP bare-IP endpoint. Behaviors observed in postinstall.js: (1) reads installer-owned secret paths including /root/.aws/credentials, /root/.ssh/, /root/.kube/config, /root/.docker/config.json, host /etc/shadow via /proc/1/root, and the Kubernetes service-account token at /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token; (2) retrieves AWS IMDSv2 tokens and EC2 instance-identity metadata from 169.254.169.254, and ECS task credentials via AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI from 169.254.170.2; (3) attempts container-escape and privilege-escalation via nsenter --target 1, chroot /proc/1/root, Docker socket abuse, and sudo-based host bind-mount/shadow reads; (4) collects os.hostname(), full process.env, and the harvested files into a report and POSTs it via curl to http://115.190.124.243:9082/callback. The package advertises itself as a 'Lightweight date formatting utility' but performs no date-formatting work — the postinstall script is the entire payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
